articles.headline: India Unveils 'National Green Hydrogen Mission - Phase II' with Ambitious Production Targets
articles.explainer:
• The government announced the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and establishing India as a global export hub.
• New targets include significantly boosting green hydrogen production capacity by 2030 and expanding electrolyser manufacturing.
• The mission aims to decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and long-haul transport, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Incentives for research and development, pilot projects, and demand creation mechanisms are key components of Phase II.
• International collaborations for technology transfer and investment are also being actively pursued to accelerate the mission's objectives.

Investment models
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation, environmental impact assessment

articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Core Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, largely upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The judgment clarified the balance between individual privacy rights and state interests, particularly concerning data processing by government entities.
• While affirming the 'right to be forgotten' and data fiduciary obligations, the court suggested minor modifications to enhance transparency mechanisms.
• Petitions challenging the broad exemptions granted to government agencies under the Act were partially addressed, with the court emphasizing robust oversight.
• This ruling sets a precedent for data governance in India, impacting digital economy, tech companies, and citizen privacy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld validity of DPDP Act, 2023.
Clarified balance between privacy rights and state interests.
Affirmed 'right to be forgotten' and data fiduciary obligations.
Emphasized robust oversight for government exemptions.
Ruling impacts digital economy and citizen privacy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on the DPDP Act for individual privacy and state surveillance in India.
Discuss how the judgment balances fundamental rights with the needs of national security and economic development.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the DPDP Act in regulating data fiduciaries and protecting data principals in the evolving digital landscape.
Examine the potential challenges in implementing the Act post-judgment and suggest measures for effective data governance.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel resting on law books with a blurred background of digital data streams.
articles.trivia: India's 'Right to Privacy' was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in 2017 in the Puttaswamy judgment.

articles.headline: RBI Monetary Policy Committee Maintains Status Quo, Signals Cautious Optimism on Inflation
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged for the sixth consecutive review.
• The MPC cited evolving inflation dynamics, global economic uncertainties, and the need to support growth as key reasons for its decision.
• While retail inflation has shown signs of moderation, the MPC highlighted concerns over volatile food prices and geopolitical risks.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting economic activity.
• Analysts anticipate future rate cuts contingent on sustained disinflation and a stable global economic environment.

Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.

mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing the RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on interest rates and its implications for the Indian economy.
Discuss the challenges faced by the MPC in balancing inflation control with the objective of supporting economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance in the current economic scenario.
Examine how global economic trends and geopolitical events impact India's monetary policy decisions.
articles.imageDescription: RBI emblem on a building facade with blurred city lights in the background.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Critical Phase Amidst Key Sector Discussions
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered a crucial stage, with both sides intensifying discussions on contentious issues.
• Key areas of focus include market access for automobiles, agricultural products, intellectual property rights, and sustainable development provisions.
• India is pushing for greater mobility for its skilled professionals, while the EU emphasizes robust data protection and environmental standards.
• A successful FTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses.
• Despite ongoing challenges, both parties express optimism about concluding a mutually beneficial agreement in the coming months.

articles.trivia: The European Union is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for 10.8% of India's total trade in goods in 2022-23.

articles.headline: New Report Warns of Accelerating Himalayan Glacier Melt, Urgent Regional Cooperation Needed
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive report by an international consortium of scientists highlights an alarming acceleration in the rate of glacier melt across the Himalayas.
• The study predicts severe consequences for water security in downstream regions, impacting billions across South Asia.
• Factors contributing to the accelerated melt include rising global temperatures, black carbon deposition, and changing precipitation patterns.
• The report calls for urgent regional cooperation among Himalayan nations to develop adaptive strategies and mitigation measures.
• Recommendations include enhancing monitoring systems, investing in climate-resilient infrastructure, and reducing local pollutant emissions.

articles.imageDescription: Panoramic view of melting glaciers in the Himalayas with a pristine blue lake.
articles.trivia: The Himalayas are often called the 'Third Pole' because they contain the largest amount of ice and snow outside of the Arctic and Antarctic.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Re-entry Vehicle for Human Spaceflight Program
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful atmospheric re-entry test of its next-generation crew module, a crucial step for the Gaganyaan mission.
• The test involved a complex maneuver to demonstrate the vehicle's ability to withstand extreme temperatures and pressures during re-entry.
• Data gathered from the test will be vital for refining the design and thermal protection systems of future human-rated spacecraft.
• This achievement significantly boosts India's capabilities in independent human spaceflight and paves the way for advanced orbital missions.
• The test also showcased indigenous technologies developed for navigation, guidance, and control systems during re-entry.

articles.imageDescription: Artist's impression of an ISRO crew module re-entering Earth's atmosphere, glowing red from friction.
articles.trivia: The Gaganyaan mission aims to send Indian astronauts to low Earth orbit on an Indian launch vehicle.

articles.headline: India Bolsters Northern Border Security with Advanced Surveillance and Integrated Systems
articles.explainer:
• India has significantly upgraded its surveillance and defense infrastructure along its northern borders, deploying advanced technologies.
• The enhancements include integrated border management systems, AI-powered cameras, drone surveillance, and sensor-based intrusion detection.
• This move aims to counter evolving security challenges, including cross-border infiltration, unauthorized movements, and hybrid threats.
• The new systems facilitate real-time intelligence gathering, faster response times, and improved coordination among security forces.
• Training programs for personnel on operating and maintaining these high-tech systems are also being intensified.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Skill India 2.0' with Focus on Green Jobs and Future Technologies
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship launched 'Skill India 2.0', an upgraded version of its flagship program.
• The new initiative places a strong emphasis on training youth for emerging 'green jobs' in renewable energy, waste management, and sustainable agriculture.
• It also incorporates skilling programs in cutting-edge fields like Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Robotics, and Data Science.
• Partnerships with industry leaders and educational institutions are central to ensuring demand-driven skill development and employment.
• The program aims to address the skill gap in India's workforce, making it future-ready and globally competitive.

articles.headline: Debate Intensifies Over Uniform Civil Code as Law Commission Submits Updated Report
articles.explainer:
• The Law Commission of India has submitted an updated report on the feasibility and implications of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) to the government.
• The report is expected to provide a comprehensive analysis of various personal laws and their potential harmonization under a UCC.
• Public discourse on the UCC has intensified, with proponents citing constitutional principles of equality and secularism, while opponents raise concerns over religious freedom and cultural diversity.
• The government is reportedly reviewing the report and assessing the socio-political readiness for such a significant legal reform.
• Legal experts anticipate a robust debate in Parliament and public sphere before any legislative action is considered.

articles.headline: Government Explores Climate-Resilient Farming Models Amidst Erratic Weather Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare is actively promoting and exploring climate-resilient farming models across various states.
• This initiative comes in response to increasing instances of erratic weather patterns, including unseasonal rains, prolonged droughts, and extreme heatwaves, impacting agricultural output.
• Focus areas include developing drought-resistant crop varieties, precision agriculture techniques, water-efficient irrigation, and agroforestry.
• Farmers are being provided with financial assistance, technical guidance, and access to climate-smart technologies.
• The long-term goal is to enhance food security, improve farmers' livelihoods, and ensure the sustainability of agricultural practices in a changing climate.

• The agenda includes discussions on strengthening global economic stability, managing inflation, and addressing rising sovereign debt in developing nations.
• India emphasized the need for coordinated multilateral action to tackle cross-border financial risks and promote inclusive growth.

articles.headline: Draft National Water Policy Emphasizes Integrated Management and Community Participation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti released the draft National Water Policy, proposing a paradigm shift towards integrated water resource management.
• The policy emphasizes treating water as a common pool resource and prioritizes community participation in planning and governance.
• Key recommendations include promoting water-use efficiency, groundwater recharge, inter-basin transfers, and addressing water quality issues.
• It advocates for a basin-level approach to water management, moving away from administrative boundaries.
• The draft policy also stresses the importance of data-driven decision-making and sustainable financing for water infrastructure projects.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Draft AI Ethics Framework for Public Consultation
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog has unveiled a draft framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics, inviting public and stakeholder feedback.
• The framework aims to guide the responsible development and deployment of AI in India, focusing on principles like fairness, transparency, accountability, and safety.
• It addresses critical issues such as data privacy, algorithmic bias, human oversight, and the societal impact of AI technologies.
• The initiative is part of India's broader strategy to become a global leader in AI while ensuring ethical considerations are paramount.
• Recommendations include establishing a national AI ethics committee and developing sector-specific guidelines for AI application.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Reports Significant Progress, Addresses Implementation Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare released a progress report on the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), highlighting its achievements and ongoing challenges.
• Significant strides have been made in creating Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs) and digitizing health records across various healthcare facilities.
• The report acknowledged hurdles such as digital literacy gaps, infrastructure disparities, and interoperability issues among different health systems.
• New initiatives are being rolled out to enhance citizen engagement, strengthen data security, and expand the network of participating healthcare providers.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Uncovers New Insights into Early Indian Urbanism
articles.explainer:

articles.headline: Lok Sabha Passes Amendments to Forest Conservation Act, Sparks Environmental Debate
articles.explainer:
• The Lok Sabha has passed significant amendments to the Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980, aimed at streamlining forest land diversion for infrastructure projects.
• The amendments propose to exempt certain categories of land from the Act's purview, including forest land along international borders and areas designated for security infrastructure.
• Environmental activists and conservationists have raised strong concerns, arguing that the changes could lead to widespread deforestation and ecological damage.
• The government maintains that the amendments are crucial for national security, development projects, and providing clarity on the applicability of the Act.
• The bill now awaits consideration in the Rajya Sabha, where further debate is anticipated.

articles.headline: Government Boosts 'Make in India' in Defence Sector with New Procurement Policies
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence has unveiled new procurement policies aimed at significantly boosting indigenous manufacturing under the 'Make in India' initiative.
• The revised Defence Acquisition Procedure (DAP) prioritizes procurement from Indian vendors and incentivizes local design, development, and production.
• Key provisions include higher domestic content requirements, reservation of certain procurement categories for Indian companies, and faster clearances for indigenous projects.
• This move seeks to reduce India's reliance on foreign defense imports, create jobs, and foster a robust domestic defense industrial base.
• The policy also encourages private sector participation, including MSMEs and startups, in defense manufacturing and R&D.

articles.headline: India Crosses Major Solar Energy Capacity Milestone, Boosts Renewable Energy Goals
articles.explainer:
• India has achieved a significant milestone by crossing a major threshold in its installed solar energy capacity, reinforcing its commitment to renewable energy.
• The achievement is attributed to robust government policies, attractive incentives for investors, and rapid technological advancements in solar power generation.
• This expansion contributes substantially to India's target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.
• The increased solar capacity is helping reduce carbon emissions, enhance energy security, and provide affordable electricity to remote areas.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'National Green Hydrogen Mission - Phase II', consider the following statements:
1. It primarily focuses on reducing India's reliance on fossil fuels in the aviation sector.
2. It aims to establish India as a global export hub for green hydrogen by significantly boosting production capacity.
3. The mission includes incentives for research and development in electrolyser manufacturing.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. While decarbonization is a goal, the primary focus of Phase II extends beyond just aviation, targeting hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and long-haul transport. Statement 2 is correct, as a key objective is to establish India as a global export hub for green hydrogen and scale up production. Statement 3 is correct, as incentives for research and development, particularly in electrolyser manufacturing, are a core component of the mission.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for submitting reports on the feasibility and implications of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) to the Indian government?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Law Commission of India is an executive body established by the Government of India for legal reform and advises the government on complex legal matters, including the Uniform Civil Code. The Supreme Court interprets laws but does not draft policy reports like this. The NHRC deals with human rights, and the Election Commission with elections.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the recent amendments to the Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980:
1. The amendments primarily aim to streamline forest land diversion for infrastructure projects.
2. They propose to exempt certain forest lands along international borders from the Act's purview.
3. Environmental activists have largely welcomed these amendments for promoting sustainable development.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct, as the amendments aim to streamline forest land diversion. Statement 2 is correct, as exemptions include forest land along international borders and for security infrastructure. Statement 3 is incorrect; environmental activists and conservationists have raised strong concerns, arguing that the changes could lead to widespread deforestation and ecological damage, not welcomed them.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA ID)' is a key component of which of the following initiatives?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA ID) is a unique digital health ID for individuals, enabling them to link and access their health records digitally. It is a core component of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), which aims to create a seamless online platform for healthcare services across the country.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic feature emphasized in the draft National Water Policy?
options: (a) Treating water as a common pool resource.
(b) Prioritizing community participation in water governance.
(c) Adopting a strict administrative boundary approach for water management.
(d) Promoting water-use efficiency and groundwater recharge.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Adopting a strict administrative boundary approach for water management.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The draft National Water Policy advocates for a basin-level approach to water management, moving away from administrative boundaries, making statement (c) incorrect. Statements (a), (b), and (d) are all key emphases of the draft policy, promoting common pool resource management, community participation, and water-use efficiency respectively.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the strategic importance of the 'National Green Hydrogen Mission - Phase II' in achieving India's energy security and climate goals. Discuss the challenges and opportunities associated with scaling up green hydrogen production to establish India as a global hub.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental pollution and degradation; Conservation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its Phase II objectives. Discuss its strategic importance for energy security (reducing import dependence) and climate goals (decarbonization of hard-to-abate sectors, achieving net-zero targets). Analyze opportunities like job creation, new industries, and export potential. Address challenges such as high production costs, technological advancements, funding, infrastructure development (transport, storage), and international competition. Conclude with policy recommendations.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's ruling on the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, marks a significant step in India's data governance framework. Discuss how this judgment balances individual privacy rights with state interests and evaluate its implications for the digital economy and citizen privacy.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution; Government policies and interventions; Role of civil services in a democracy.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the context of the DPDP Act and the Supreme Court's ruling. Explain the 'right to privacy' as a fundamental right. Analyze how the judgment attempts to balance individual privacy with state interests (e.g., national security, public order), particularly concerning government exemptions. Discuss implications for the digital economy (e.g., tech companies, data fiduciaries) and for citizens (e.g., data principal rights, 'right to be forgotten'). Conclude with the need for robust oversight and transparent implementation.

mainsMocks.question: Erratic weather patterns pose significant challenges to India's agricultural sector, impacting food security and farmers' livelihoods. In this context, critically evaluate the government's initiatives to promote climate-resilient farming models and suggest further measures for their widespread adoption.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Major crops cropping patterns; Food security; Environmental pollution and degradation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the vulnerability of Indian agriculture to climate change and erratic weather. Detail the government's initiatives for climate-resilient farming (e.g., drought-resistant crops, precision agriculture, agroforestry, financial assistance). Critically evaluate their effectiveness, highlighting successes and limitations (e.g., awareness, access to technology, financial viability for small farmers). Suggest further measures like enhanced R&D, extension services, market linkages, insurance schemes, and community-based adaptation strategies. Conclude with the importance of a holistic approach.

GS1.headline: New Report Warns of Accelerating Himalayan Glacier Melt, Urgent Regional Cooperation Needed
GS1.explainer:
• A comprehensive report by an international consortium of scientists highlights an alarming acceleration in the rate of glacier melt across the Himalayas.
• The study predicts severe consequences for water security in downstream regions, impacting billions across South Asia.
• Factors contributing to the accelerated melt include rising global temperatures, black carbon deposition, and changing precipitation patterns.
• The report calls for urgent regional cooperation among Himalayan nations to develop adaptive strategies and mitigation measures.
• Recommendations include enhancing monitoring systems, investing in climate-resilient infrastructure, and reducing local pollutant emissions.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Core Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, largely upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The judgment clarified the balance between individual privacy rights and state interests, particularly concerning data processing by government entities.
• While affirming the 'right to be forgotten' and data fiduciary obligations, the court suggested minor modifications to enhance transparency mechanisms.
• Petitions challenging the broad exemptions granted to government agencies under the Act were partially addressed, with the court emphasizing robust oversight.
• This ruling sets a precedent for data governance in India, impacting digital economy, tech companies, and citizen privacy.

GS3.headline: India Unveils 'National Green Hydrogen Mission - Phase II' with Ambitious Production Targets
GS3.explainer:
• The government announced the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and establishing India as a global export hub.
• New targets include significantly boosting green hydrogen production capacity by 2030 and expanding electrolyser manufacturing.
• The mission aims to decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and long-haul transport, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Incentives for research and development, pilot projects, and demand creation mechanisms are key components of Phase II.
• International collaborations for technology transfer and investment are also being actively pursued to accelerate the mission's objectives.

GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

GS3.headline: RBI Monetary Policy Committee Maintains Status Quo, Signals Cautious Optimism on Inflation
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged for the sixth consecutive review.
• The MPC cited evolving inflation dynamics, global economic uncertainties, and the need to support growth as key reasons for its decision.
• While retail inflation has shown signs of moderation, the MPC highlighted concerns over volatile food prices and geopolitical risks.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting economic activity.
• Analysts anticipate future rate cuts contingent on sustained disinflation and a stable global economic environment.

GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Re-entry Vehicle for Human Spaceflight Program
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful atmospheric re-entry test of its next-generation crew module, a crucial step for the Gaganyaan mission.
• The test involved a complex maneuver to demonstrate the vehicle's ability to withstand extreme temperatures and pressures during re-entry.
• Data gathered from the test will be vital for refining the design and thermal protection systems of future human-rated spacecraft.
• This achievement significantly boosts India's capabilities in independent human spaceflight and paves the way for advanced orbital missions.
• The test also showcased indigenous technologies developed for navigation, guidance, and control systems during re-entry.

GS3.trivia: The Gaganyaan mission aims to send Indian astronauts to low Earth orbit on an Indian launch vehicle.

GS3.headline: India Bolsters Northern Border Security with Advanced Surveillance and Integrated Systems
GS3.explainer:
• India has significantly upgraded its surveillance and defense infrastructure along its northern borders, deploying advanced technologies.
• The enhancements include integrated border management systems, AI-powered cameras, drone surveillance, and sensor-based intrusion detection.
• This move aims to counter evolving security challenges, including cross-border infiltration, unauthorized movements, and hybrid threats.
• The new systems facilitate real-time intelligence gathering, faster response times, and improved coordination among security forces.
• Training programs for personnel on operating and maintaining these high-tech systems are also being intensified.

GS3.headline: Government Explores Climate-Resilient Farming Models Amidst Erratic Weather Challenges
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare is actively promoting and exploring climate-resilient farming models across various states.
• This initiative comes in response to increasing instances of erratic weather patterns, including unseasonal rains, prolonged droughts, and extreme heatwaves, impacting agricultural output.
• Focus areas include developing drought-resistant crop varieties, precision agriculture techniques, water-efficient irrigation, and agroforestry.
• Farmers are being provided with financial assistance, technical guidance, and access to climate-smart technologies.
• The long-term goal is to enhance food security, improve farmers' livelihoods, and ensure the sustainability of agricultural practices in a changing climate.

GS3.headline: Draft National Water Policy Emphasizes Integrated Management and Community Participation
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti released the draft National Water Policy, proposing a paradigm shift towards integrated water resource management.
• The policy emphasizes treating water as a common pool resource and prioritizes community participation in planning and governance.
• Key recommendations include promoting water-use efficiency, groundwater recharge, inter-basin transfers, and addressing water quality issues.
• It advocates for a basin-level approach to water management, moving away from administrative boundaries.
• The draft policy also stresses the importance of data-driven decision-making and sustainable financing for water infrastructure projects.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Draft AI Ethics Framework for Public Consultation
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog has unveiled a draft framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics, inviting public and stakeholder feedback.
• The framework aims to guide the responsible development and deployment of AI in India, focusing on principles like fairness, transparency, accountability, and safety.
• It addresses critical issues such as data privacy, algorithmic bias, human oversight, and the societal impact of AI technologies.
• The initiative is part of India's broader strategy to become a global leader in AI while ensuring ethical considerations are paramount.
• Recommendations include establishing a national AI ethics committee and developing sector-specific guidelines for AI application.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts 'Make in India' in Defence Sector with New Procurement Policies
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence has unveiled new procurement policies aimed at significantly boosting indigenous manufacturing under the 'Make in India' initiative.
• The revised Defence Acquisition Procedure (DAP) prioritizes procurement from Indian vendors and incentivizes local design, development, and production.
• Key provisions include higher domestic content requirements, reservation of certain procurement categories for Indian companies, and faster clearances for indigenous projects.
• This move seeks to reduce India's reliance on foreign defense imports, create jobs, and foster a robust domestic defense industrial base.
• The policy also encourages private sector participation, including MSMEs and startups, in defense manufacturing and R&D.

GS3.headline: India Crosses Major Solar Energy Capacity Milestone, Boosts Renewable Energy Goals
GS3.explainer:
• India has achieved a significant milestone by crossing a major threshold in its installed solar energy capacity, reinforcing its commitment to renewable energy.
• The achievement is attributed to robust government policies, attractive incentives for investors, and rapid technological advancements in solar power generation.
• This expansion contributes substantially to India's target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.
• The increased solar capacity is helping reduce carbon emissions, enhance energy security, and provide affordable electricity to remote areas.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, energy storage, and land acquisition, which are being addressed through policy interventions and R&D.
